summary: postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud/SCM configuration and exfiltrates to hardcoded endpoint
details: On npm install, scripts/postinstall.js reads a wide set of installer-side identity and configuration files — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, project.git/config and logs/HEAD (git reflog author emails), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (GitHub CLI login/email), ~/.ssh/*.pub (SSH key comment emails), ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP project/account), ~/.aws/config (AWS profile names), /etc/resolv.conf, and parent package.json — along with os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and os.platform(). The collected fields (scmEmail, githubLogin, sshKeyEmails, gcpProject, gcpAccount, awsProfiles, recentAuthors, remoteOrigin, hostname, username) are POSTed as JSON via https.request to the hardcoded host npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The destination is an author-controlled Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the package's declared PostgreSQL MCP server purpose; the 'telemetry' framing does not equal installer consent, and installer-owned secret directories (~/.aws, ~/.ssh, ~/.config/gcloud, ~/.config/gh) are enumerated. This is a developer-identity harvest that supports follow-on targeting of the installer's SCM and cloud accounts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
